legacyHotspots.rationale: The composition root wires runtime owners, window presentation, coordinators, worker adapters, and IPC; this strict cap prevents product workflows from returning.
The renderer root is now a composition shell for feature-owned models and views; this strict cap prevents it from reabsorbing feature state and policy.
The public SshHost owns one authentication, connection, cache, and capacity policy while delegating file, watch, and transport-pool mechanics to private collaborators.
TerminalWorkspace is now the feature composition view over extracted policy, effects, persistence, recovery, and presentation modules; the named cap preserves that boundary.
HarnessProfilesSettings composes extracted profile policy, controller, fields, and dialogs; the named cap prevents those responsibilities from moving back into the view.
GitEngine is the stable facade over capability modules and the shared command context; its lower named cap prevents the facade from reabsorbing capability implementations.
The root stylesheet is an explicit ordered import manifest; its lower named cap prevents feature rules from returning to the global composition surface.
Legacy end-to-end SshHost adapter characterization remains in one test module while new collaborator policy is tested in focused modules.
The remaining transitional renderer workflow stays production-composed while focused PTY, viewer-position, and packaged platform contracts run independently.
